summary: Verified security validations for Database Hard/Soft Reset (frontend modals + backend endpoints) and Historic months endpoint. Both Hard Reset (4 fields: current pwd, new pwd, repeat pwd, RESETEAR text) and Soft Reset (current pwd + 5 checkboxes: Ventas, Usuarios, Inventario A, Inventario B, Clientes) render correctly. Backend correctly rejects wrong password with 401 on both endpoints.
passed: Admin login (admin / Admin3b4e892c) succeeds and returns JWT
Settings -> Base de Datos tab renders Hard Reset and Soft Reset sections
Hard Reset modal shows exactly 4 required fields with correct placeholders (Contraseña Actual, Nueva Contraseña, Repetir, RESETEAR text field)
Hard Reset 'Confirmar Reset' button is disabled until all fields valid
Soft Reset modal shows password field + 5 checkboxes (Ventas, Usuarios, Inventario Tienda A, Inventario Tienda B, Clientes)
Soft Reset 'Confirmar Reset' button disabled until valid
POST /api/database/hard-reset with wrong password returns 401 {detail: 'Contraseña incorrecta'}
POST /api/database/soft-reset with wrong password returns 401 {detail: 'Contraseña incorrecta'}
GET /api/dashboard/historic-months returns 200 with array of {year, month}

failed.issue: Soft-reset endpoint accepts correct password with all options=false and returns 200 success (empty deleted_counts). Validation of at-least-one-option is enforced only in frontend. Not a blocker since UI prevents this, but a defense-in-depth gap.
failed.evidence: curl POST /api/database/soft-reset with password OK and all flags false -> HTTP 200 {"success":true,"deleted_counts":{}}

failed.issue: Hard Reset and Soft Reset modal inputs and confirm/cancel buttons lack data-testid attributes, making automated selection fragile (had to rely on placeholders/text).
failed.evidence: input elements returned testid=None for all password / RESETEAR fields

action_items: (Optional) Add server-side validation for soft-reset to require at least one option flag=true.
(Optional) Add data-testid attributes to Hard/Soft Reset modal fields and confirm buttons for robust testing.
notes: Did not execute a destructive Hard Reset to avoid rotating admin credentials during test session. Historic Month Selector loading/error UI states not visually reproduced because the endpoint responded quickly with success; loading spinner code is present in RealtimeMetrics.js per problem statement but was not observed under failure condition. Recommend simulating a slow/failing response in follow-up full-testing pass.
